children.name: PlaceOrder
children.purpose: Execute the complete place order workflow: validate stock, charge payment, reserve inventory, create order record, send notification.
children.behavior: Calls validation, payment, inventory, order creation, and notification functions in sequence with rollback on failure.

semantic_inputs.name: order_data
semantic_inputs.description: Order payload with items, payment_method, shipping_address

semantic_outputs.name: order_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result with success, order_id, status, message

postconditions: If success, order record created with status 'confirmed'
If failure, no side effects remain

children.name: CancelOrder
children.purpose: Execute the cancel order workflow: verify order exists and not shipped, refund payment, restore inventory, update order status.
children.behavior: Calls verification, refund, inventory restoration, and status update functions in sequence with rollback on failure.

in_scope: verifying order exists and not shipped

semantic_outputs.name: order_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result with success, order_id, status, message

postconditions: If success, order status set to 'cancelled'
If failure, no side effects remain

children.name: TrackOrder
children.purpose: Return current order status and estimated delivery time for a given order.
children.behavior: Looks up order by order_id in orders data store and returns status and estimated delivery time.

semantic_outputs.name: order_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result with success, order_id, status, message including delivery time

postconditions: Order data is read, not modified

decomposition_rationale: The parent OrderSystem function dispatches based on command to three independent child functions, each handling a complete workflow for one command. This keeps children at the same abstraction level (order-level operations) and avoids overlapping responsibilities.
